methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy

data.narrative:
Calogena is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Frédéric Mariotte that has raised €100M to date. The valuation exercise draws from a comp universe of 11 nuclear energy peers: 3 publicly traded entities (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private companies, including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

The peer-median method proved inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables. While 8 same-sector private peers exist, filtering for same-stage or adjacent-stage companies with similar valuation tiers yielded fewer than 3 datapoints—below the minimum threshold for reliable median calculation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce excessive heterogeneity and risk misleading results given the specialized capital intensity and technical differentiation within nuclear energy.

Given these constraints, a defensible valuation range cannot be established through triangulation at this time. Further progress requires either: (1) expansion of the Series A nuclear cohort through additional fundraising announcements or private equity data; (2) reliance on public market multiples (EV/revenue, EV/EBITDA) from the three listed peers, though direct application to early-stage private equity remains challenged by profitability and revenue divergence; or (3) explicit DCF modeling incorporating Calogena's specific technical pathway and commercialization timeline. The €100M raised establishes a floor for investor conviction but does not constitute valuation. Key uncertainties include regulatory pathway clarity and capital requirements to commercialization.
